message:
test: harden two repaired tests against fresh-eyes review concerns

Self-review of commits d037c00a (oauth redact-test repair) and 3e2307da
(pre-existing failure repair) found two genuine weaknesses in the
fixes themselves. Both are addressed here without touching production
behavior.

1. fcp-streaming: ws_connection_recv_times_out_when_peer_blackholes_after_handshake

   The repair landed `connect_timeout = 100ms` to make `io_timeout()`'s
   `connect_timeout.max(...)` floor narrow enough that the timeout would
   actually fire before the server's TCP close. But 100ms is the same
   value used as the dial-step timeout: on a loaded CI runner, the TCP
   + HTTP-upgrade handshake roundtrip can plausibly stretch past 100ms,
   and `client.connect().await` would then fail with `Err(Timeout)`
   before recv ever runs. That re-introduces flakiness — exactly what
   the original repair was meant to remove.

   Fix: bump `connect_timeout` from 100ms to 300ms (≥3x typical local
   dial latency, comfortable headroom for shared CI), and bump the
   server stall from 500ms to 1500ms so the timeout window has 5x
   margin to fire before TCP close. The assertion still checks
   `connect_timeout.max(pong_timeout)` — the test's invariant is
   unchanged, only the absolute numbers got more tolerant.

   Verified: 5 consecutive runs of the test all pass with consistent
   ~1.5s wall time. No production code touched.

2. fcp-oauth: test_request_token_debug

   The repair used multi-character sentinel values
   `request_token_sentinel_value_zzz` and
   `request_token_secret_sentinel_zzz`. Both share the prefix
   `request_token_` — and a buggy Debug impl that truncates field values
   (e.g. emits the first 16 bytes for "log compactness") would leak
   `request_token_se` from one field, which contains the prefix
   `request_token_` of the OTHER sentinel. The `!contains(other)`
   assertion would still pass, missing a real partial leak.

   Two improvements:

   a) Sentinel design: use truly unrelated string shapes per field —
      `alpha-marauder-3F2A1B0E` for `token` and `delta-pickle-9C7D6E5F`
      for `token_secret`. No shared substring with each other or with
      any field name (`token`, `RequestToken`, `[REDACTED]`,
      `callback_confirmed`). A truncated leak from either field
      cannot satisfy both `!contains(...)` checks.

   b) Redaction-count assertion: explicitly require the Debug output
      contain `[REDACTED]` AT LEAST TWICE. A buggy Debug that
      redacts `token` but accidentally leaves `token_secret` un-
      redacted would have only one `[REDACTED]` marker — caught by
      `debug.matches("[REDACTED]").count() >= 2`. The earlier single
      `contains("[REDACTED]")` was unable to distinguish "one field
      redacted" from "both fields redacted".

   Doc-comment on the test names both pitfalls so a future refactor
   cannot regress to the broken shape.

   Verified: cargo test -p fcp-oauth --lib — 623/623 pass, including
   the hardened test.

3. State-mismatch + quarantine repairs (commits d037c00a / 3e2307da):
   reviewed and confirmed no equivalent weaknesses. The Display
   assertion is exact-equality on a constant (matches the canonical
   non-empty-strings test in the same file) and the quarantine tests
   correctly model the in-band sweep boundary (`age > ttl`) so a
   future change to `>=` would be caught by both tests flipping their
   expected counts.
